title: Add strict per-command flag and positional validation to bench before any side-effecting dispatch
problem: Generic parseArgs accepts every --flag, and most dispatch arms never reject unused flags. Unsupported safety flags such as --dry-run and unknown flags such as --jsno can be silently dropped while detached processes launch.
evidence: probe: bun ui/bench.ts eval-run --suite evals/suites/smoke.json --dry-run exited 0 and launched a harness
implementation_hint: Derive allowed flags and arity from command metadata or a new command schema. Reject unknown flags and unexpected positionals with bench-error.v1 JSON before calling startEvalRun/startOptimizeRun/startRun.

title: Expose a safe eval-run robot path through the primary bench CLI
problem: Agents naturally try --dry-run on eval-run, but bench currently ignores it and starts the live detached harness. The direct runner has a good --robot-dry-run path that the primary CLI does not surface or forward.
evidence: probe: bench eval-run --dry-run launched a detached process
implementation_hint: Support bench eval-run --robot-dry-run/--dry-run/--replay/--json-summary by calling the runner synchronously, or reject those flags with a precise JSON error and next_command pointing at uv run harness/runner/run_eval.py --robot-dry-run.
tests: new bench eval-run robot dry-run contract test

title: Add did-you-mean recovery for unknown bench commands and flags
problem: Unknown commands return JSON usage but no correction. Unknown flags should become structured errors with suggestions once strict validation lands.
evidence: probe: bun ui/bench.ts evalrun ... returned no did_you_mean
implementation_hint: Use edit distance and prefix matching against COMMAND_DETAILS. Include did_you_mean and next_command fields in bench-error.v1.
tests: unknown command typo tests for evalrun/evalruns/workflowrun

title: Make runner robot-mode failures JSON from argument parsing through suite loading
problem: The success path emits eval-dry-run-summary.v1, but argparse conflicts, unknown flags, suite errors, and empty matrices are prose. Stored output cannot be consumed uniformly by agents.
implementation_hint: Add a parser/error wrapper for --robot-dry-run or any argv containing --json-summary. Emit eval-error.v1 with phase, exit_code, message, and suggested_command.

title: Give gen_eval_cases validate/promote stable JSON schemas and clearer status semantics
problem: Validate-only emits ad hoc JSON without schema_version, and ok:true can coexist with sensitivity_status:fail. Promote returns mixed prose/JSON and lacks a schema contract.
evidence: probe: validate-only returned ok true with sensitivity_status fail
implementation_hint: Define case-validation.v1 and case-promote-result.v1 schemas. Include aggregate ok, per-gate statuses, exit_code, and whether sensitivity failures are advisory or blocking.
tests: schema validation tests for validate-only and promote dry failures

title: Harden promote with rollback-on-exception, atomic suite writes, and bounded post-checks
problem: Promote mutates case directories and suite files, but rollback is problem-driven rather than exception-safe, and post-check subprocesses can hang indefinitely.
implementation_hint: Wrap mutation phase in try/except/finally, write suite updates through temp file plus atomic replace, and pass a timeout to post-promote check subprocesses.

title: Enrich repo-check-result.v1 with next_commands, exit_code, and failure kind
problem: Check scripts are parseable but under-prescriptive. Agents must infer whether a JSON fail is a usage error or validation debt, and they do not receive a next command.
evidence: subagent finding: argparse JSON payload has status fail but no kind
implementation_hint: Add optional fields while preserving repo-check-result.v1 compatibility, or version to repo-check-result.v2. Include validation_failure versus usage_error and suggested rerun commands.

title: Improve detached-process recovery metadata and status filtering
problem: Detached launch responses and eval-runs output can be too volatile or large for agent loops. eval-runs returned a very large payload during audit, and live launch success can precede asynchronous spawn failure.
evidence: ui/lib.ts:1556-1578 sidecar includes pid and startedAtMs
subagent finding: detached spawn errors may report success
probe: eval-runs payload was large enough to require filtering
implementation_hint: Preflight executables before success, include next_commands, add eval-runs --running/--limit/--json-lines filters, and mark volatile fields in capabilities.

title: Bound and filter bench eval-runs output for agent loops
problem: The primary run-listing command can return too much historical run data for an agent status loop, and agents lack a compact running-only or line-delimited stream.
evidence: subagent finding: eval-runs payload was large enough to require filtering
probe: bun ui/bench.ts eval-runs had no --running/--limit/--json-lines before pass 3
implementation_hint: Add --status, --running, --limit, and --json-lines, return a schema-versioned bounded envelope, and document next commands.

title: Accept top-level help and common global machine-mode flags
problem: Agents commonly start with --help or prefix commands with --json, but bench rejected top-level --help and treated --json as a command even though output is already JSON.
evidence: subagent finding: bun ui/bench.ts --help failed as unknown command
subagent finding: bun ui/bench.ts --json doctor failed despite JSON default
implementation_hint: Special-case top-level --help and strip global no-op flags before command dispatch while advertising them in capabilities.

title: Support --flag=value in generic and bespoke bench parsers
problem: Several agent and shell generators emit --flag=value, but bench only supported separated --flag value syntax for many options.
evidence: subagent finding: --project=. and similar inline flags were rejected
probe: eval-case-generate needed bespoke parser support
implementation_hint: Normalize long options through a shared parser helper, preserve duplicate-scalar checks, and reject inline values on boolean flags.

title: Standardize bench-owned errors on bench-error.v1
problem: Bench-owned command and validation failures used inconsistent JSON shapes, making retry and status handling harder for agents.
evidence: subagent finding: inconsistent CLI error envelopes
probe: unknown command error lacked schema_version/status/exit_code
implementation_hint: Emit bench-error.v1 with status and exit_code for bench-owned errors, while preserving wrapped subprocess schemas that are already more specific.

title: Accept a skill directory path for first-run eval-case generation
problem: The natural first command for a brand-new skill is to pass its filesystem path, but gen_eval_cases.py previously only accepted a repo-local skill name under skills/<name>. New skills with no evals therefore required a separate import/onboarding step before case generation could even start.
evidence: probe expectation: uv run harness/generate/gen_eval_cases.py --skill /path/to/skill --n 3 should import/use the skill and enter bootstrap context
implementation_hint: Resolve --skill before SkillContext as either a repo skill name or a full skill directory path. Import the full directory into skills/<frontmatter-name> when the repo copy is missing.

title: Infer bootstrap context for true zero-case generation
problem: Pass 4 preserved bootstrap for validate/promote, but generation itself still risked failing before the LM path with 'no loadable eval cases to seed from' unless the agent knew to add --bootstrap.
implementation_hint: Treat a skill with no visible eval case directories as bootstrap context for generation, validate-only, and promote, unless a seed example is explicitly requested.

title: Import the full skill directory and record provenance
problem: A skill is not just SKILL.md; references, schemas, scripts, and assets can be required to bootstrap credible eval cases. Path import also needs provenance so agents can tell which repo skill name was generated from which source path.
evidence: user clarification: skill can have supporting directories; better primary path is skill directory
implementation_hint: Copy the full skill directory, ignore only obvious local/build junk, accept SKILL.md file paths only as an alias for the parent directory, and include source/import metadata in generation config/report.

title: Advertise the first-run name-or-path workflow through bench help and docs
problem: Even if the generator supports skill paths, agents will keep using only --skill <name> if capabilities/help/docs do not surface the path form and zero-case bootstrap behavior.
implementation_hint: Change eval-case-generate usage to --skill <name-or-path>, document skill-directory path as the primary path form, and note that SKILL.md is only an alias for its parent directory.

title: Synthesize a minimal Laguna contract for imported prompt-style skills
problem: Real skills under ~/.agents/skills, including philip, can have scripts and references but no Laguna schemas or validate_*.ts validators. Path import worked, but the generator previously stopped instead of adding the missing harness layer and continuing.
evidence: real probe: /home/ben/.agents/skills/philip imports with Workflows/, docs/, scripts/, then lacks scripts/validate_*.ts
implementation_hint: For path-imported skills lacking validators, generate a minimal .laguna/<skill>.json output contract, JSON schema, and validate_<skill>_synthetic_bootstrap.ts on the imported repo copy before loading SkillContext.

title: Report LM setup failures without Python tracebacks
problem: After synthetic Laguna bootstrap, a missing API key can still surface as a raw RuntimeError traceback, which makes the first-run flow look crashed instead of waiting for model credentials or validate/promote inputs.
evidence: real philip probe with --api-key-env __GEN_EVAL_CASES_REAL_SMOKE_MISSING_KEY__ previously leaked a Traceback
implementation_hint: Catch make_lm RuntimeError during generation setup and print a concise stderr error plus next actions.
